gold_change_reason: The scaled task requires multi-line parsing, always returning a list of dicts, and raising ValueError on missing/malformed fields.
covered_requirements: Modify the input file to contain multiple lines (each a separate tumor sample), requiring the function to parse all lines and return aggregated results.

Add a parameter to the function that toggles output format between single sample dict (default for one line) and list of dicts (for multiple lines). However, to make it simpler, change the required output to always be a list of dicts (even for one line). This increases constraint on output structure.
Add constraint that the function must raise a ValueError if any required field is missing or malformed (e.g., non-numeric NLL, missing mu, incorrect number of mu components).
implementation_notes: Function always returns a list of dictionaries, one per data line, regardless of number of lines.
Parsing logic preserved from seed, including handling of placeholders (alphabetic for C/p*).
Validation added: empty file, missing fields, non-numeric NLL, insufficient mu components, non-numeric mu fractions all raise ValueError.
